news.summary: SOS Limited reported a 92.6% surge in commodity trading revenue to $214.3 million in FY 2024, with cryptocurrency mining revenue halving to $9.3 million due to facility upgrades in Texas. Revenue from hosting services and other segments also decreased. The company holds 736.75 units of BTC and 2,924.79 units of ETH, focusing on product lines like sesame, sulfur, rubber, and more. Revenue costs increased by $146.2 million, and operating expenses rose by $9.6 million in 2024. Selling expenses increased to $2.8 million, general and administrative expenses rose by $7.1 million, mainly due to mining rig depreciation. SOS is transitioning towards commodity trading, with its services breakdown showing a focus on this area at 92.6%. Operating expenses increased by 50.6% year-on-year, while revenue sources included commodity trading, cryptocurrency mining, hosting services, and other segments, with commodity trading representing 92.6% of total revenue. SOS is resuming full production after facility upgrades.
news.headline: SOS Limited's FY 2024 sees 92.6% surge in commodity trading revenue to $214.3M; cryptocurrency mining halves to $9.3M. Expenses increase significantly, with revenue costs growing to $224.4M and operating expenses rising to $28.6M. Operating income affected by upgrades and expansions, yet SOS remains firm on its strategic pivot towards commodity trading. SOS holds 736.75 units of BTC and 2,924.79 units of ETH as of Dec 31, 2024.

news.summary: Safe Pro Group Inc. (Nasdaq: SPAI) based in AVENTURA, Fla., which offers artificial intelligence (AI)-driven security solutions, has released its corporate update for the first quarter of 2025. The period signifies a significant shift for Safe Pro as it has started to establish strong connections with U.S. government officials. The Safe Pro Object Threat Detection platform has garnered substantial attention, leading to the preparation of various joint bids. This uptick in interest has set the stage for potential growth opportunities for the company.
news.headline: Safe Pro Group reports Q1 2025 results, gaining traction with U.S. govt. stakeholders; interest in Safe Pro Object Threat Detection increases.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh, Wabtec Corporation (NYSE: WAB) revealed its Board of Directors' decision to issue a standard quarterly common dividend of 25 cents per share on June 10, 2025, to shareholders of record on May 27, 2025. Wabtec Corporation, a prominent global supplier in the freight and transit rail sectors, focuses on innovative equipment, systems, digital solutions, and additional services to transform transportation for upcoming generations.
news.headline: Wabtec (NYSE: WAB) declared a 25 cents per share quarterly common dividend, payable on June 10, 2025, to holders of record on May 27, 2025.

news.summary: Ash Pampati, head of ecosystem at the Aptos Foundation, believes that stablecoin regulation will be the next big driver for the crypto industry, attracting institutional investors. Pampati highlighted how stablecoins, particularly outside the US, are gaining traction for cross-border payments due to their efficiency and lower costs compared to traditional methods. A survey by Fireblocks shows that 71% of respondents in Latin America use stablecoins for cross-border payments, with 50% expecting lower transaction costs. 86% of firms are ready to adopt stablecoins, with 75% seeing clear customer demand. Despite rising confidence in stablecoins, regulatory barriers remain a factor, though progress is being made globally with regulations like the EU's MiCA and the US's GENIUS Act gaining attention. This shift towards regulation and adoption of stablecoins could revolutionize the fintech space and attract more institutional investors.
news.headline: Stablecoin regulation seen as key catalyst for crypto industry, with 86% of firms ready for adoption, driving institutional interest.

news.summary: An announcement was made by Camping World Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CWH), headquartered in Lincolnshire, Illinois, declaring a regular cash dividend of $0.125 per share for the Company’s Class A Common Stock. The payment is set to be made on June 27, 2025, to stockholders of record as of June 13, 2025. The Board of Directors of Camping World will continue to assess and decide on future quarterly dividends.
news.headline: Camping World declares $0.125 dividend per share for Class A Common Stockholders on June 27, 2025; record date June 13.

news.summary: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc. disclosed that it is set to release its financial results for the first quarter of fiscal 2025, covering the period ending May 3, 2025. The announcement is scheduled to take place on Thursday, May 29, 2025, before the commencement of trading on the New York Stock Exchange. The company plans to conduct its quarterly investor conference call to delve into the outcomes at 9 a.m. ET on the same day. The conference call can be joined live at (201) 493-6780 for international callers or (877) 407-3982 toll-free.
news.headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop will report Q1 fiscal 2025 results on May 29, 2025, with an investor call at 9am ET. Dial-in: (201) 493-6780 or (877) 407-3982.

news.summary: Molecular Partners AG, a biotech company, announced key milestones and financial results for Q1 2025. They have CHF 131 million in cash as of March 31, 2025, funding operations until 2027. Expected milestones in 2025 include an IND filing and initial clinical data for their Radio-DARPin therapy program, MP0712. Data from dosing cohort 8 in the MP0533 trial show positive responses, and dosing cohort 9 has been initiated. They expanded a strategic partnership with Orano Med to co-develop up to ten radiotherapy programs. Preclinical data for MP0712 show promising results, with plans for an IND application in mid-2025 and a Phase 1 study in the second half of 2025. They will also provide MP0712 for compassionate use in South Africa. The company aims to deliver results that validate their science and create value for patients and stakeholders.
news.headline: Molecular Partners' Q1 2025 highlights: CHF 131 million cash position, two milestones expected this year, data on MP0712 & MP0533.

news.summary: SolarBank Corporation reported its fiscal third quarter and fiscal 2025 interim financial results. In the nine-month period ended March 31, 2025, IPP revenue increased from $0.3 million to $6.6 million, with gross profit at $5.8 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $0.02 million, a decrease from $2.3 million in 2024. Assets grew 395% to $194 million due to an acquisition. Revenues were $29.1 million, down from $50.4 million in 2024. Cash flow from operating activities decreased to $(2.1) million, and there was a net loss of $9 million, compared to a net income of $5.5 million in 2024. The company announced projects and partnerships, including a $7.3 million sale of the Camillus Solar Project and a $25.8 million RBC-backed battery energy storage project. SolarBank's President and CEO, Dr. Richard Lu, discussed the company's growth strategy and the $100 million CIM Transaction. The company finished the third quarter of fiscal 2025 with $45.3 million in current assets.
news.headline: SolarBank reported fiscal third quarter results: Revenue $29.1M, Net loss $9M, Cash flow -$2.1M, Assets $194M, IPP revenue $6.6M, and EBITDA $23.4K.

news.summary: Trinity Biotech plc, a biotechnology company, reported its results for Q4 2024 and FY 2024, showing total revenues of $15.9m and $61.6m respectively. Key developments included progress on profitability initiatives, consolidation of manufacturing activities, and successful completion of some site consolidations. The company experienced a net loss of $17.0m in Q4 2024, an increase in losses compared to Q4 2023. Operating profit was $21.4m in FY 2024, representing a gross margin of 34.8%. Trinity Biotech also announced entering an amended credit agreement to enhance liquidity and support its transformation plan. Key financial figures included a cash balance of $5.2m at the end of Q4 2024, investing cash outflows of $2.7m during the quarter, and aggregate gross proceeds of $8.3m from an At the Market Offering Agreement. The company's website is www.trinitybiotech.com.
news.headline: Trinity Biotech announces Q4 2024 revenue of $15.9m, up 18.1% from previous year, with losses increasing to $17.0m. Annual revenue for 2024 is $61.6m, with total loss amounting to $31.8m. The company entered into an amended credit agreement with Perceptive Advisors to enhance liquidity. More details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Bank of America has recently received a Relative Strength Rating upgrade, indicating a positive trend in its technical performance. This improvement in rating suggests a rebound for Bank of America as it continues to strengthen. The stock's upgrade reflects its potential for further growth in the market.
news.headline: Bank of America's stock earns rating upgrade, shows improving technical performance amid rebound.

news.summary: Septerna, Inc. announces a global collaboration with Novo Nordisk to develop oral small molecules for metabolic diseases, with over $2.2 billion in potential milestone payments, including more than $200 million upfront. They are on track to select a next-generation oral small molecule PTH1R agonist by the end of 2025. Additionally, they are set to initiate a Phase 1 trial for their SEP-631 MRGPRX2 NAM program in the third quarter of 2025. They ended the first quarter of 2025 with $398.2 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities. The collaboration is expected to significantly extend their financial runway. Septerna aims to create long-term value for patients and shareholders with their diverse drug pipeline. They are also working on programs for Graves' disease and thyroid eye disease. The collaboration will include R&D programs for small molecule therapies targeting GPCR receptors for obesity, type 2 diabetes, and other metabolic diseases. The agreement also includes royalty payments on global net sales of products. Financially, R&D expenses were $19.3 million, G&A expenses were $6.9 million, and net loss was $21.5 million for the quarter ended March 31, 2025. Septerna aims to pioneer GPCR drug discovery with its proprietary platform.
news.headline: Septerna reports $398.2M in cash, and collaboration with Novo Nordisk for $2.2B; multiple development programs underway.

news.summary: Coinbase Global has seen an increase in their Relative Strength Rating, indicating improved technical performance. This upgrade highlights the growth of the company in terms of market dynamics and investor sentiment. The positive shift in their RS Rating is a significant indicator of their stock's potential strength and performance in the market.
news.headline: Coinbase Global Stock gets Relative Strength Rating upgrade, indicating better technical performance.

The United States alone has recorded over 25 million cases with a death toll exceeding 400,000. Authorities are racing to distribute vaccines as quickly as possible to combat the pandemic. In economic news, the stock market experienced a slight dip with the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling by 100 points. Experts are closely monitoring the situation for any potential impact on the overall financial stability.
news.headline: Trump and allies support unlimited AI growth, per reports.

news.summary: GameStop Cl A has received a Relative Strength Rating boost, indicating enhanced technical performance. The question now arises whether this positive trend will persist. This increase in RS rating suggests potential future growth for GameStop stock.
news.headline: GameStop's Relative Strength Rating improves, indicating better technical performance. Will the uptrend continue?

news.summary: 374Water Inc. reported its financial and operational results for Q1 2025, focusing on its AirSCWO waste destruction technology. It had revenue of $0.5 million, up from $0.3 million in the previous year, mainly from equipment manufacturing and services. Total operating expenses increased to $3.9 million, resulting in a net loss of $3.7 million. Cash and equivalents were $6.9 million on March 31, 2025. The company made progress in further optimizing AirSCWO and appointed new executive team members. Multiple deployments are scheduled throughout 2025 to showcase the technology, including a contract to destroy 1,000 gallons of per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances for North Carolina. Upcoming events include establishing waste destruction services, completing demonstrations, and deploying systems. The company is optimistic about increased demand due to regulatory actions on PFAS contamination. Gannon highlighted the significant commercial progress and executive team growth, anticipating additional milestones ahead. The company's AirSCWO technology is aimed at efficiently destroying and mineralizing various organic wastes, helping customers meet discharge requirements and reduce disposal costs. Gannon and Kline will host a conference call to discuss the financial results.
news.headline: 374Water reported Q1 2025 financial results, with $0.5M revenue, 106% opex increase to $3.9M, $3.7M net loss, and $6.9M cash.

news.summary: Outlook Therapeutics, Inc. reported financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2025, focusing on their planned commercial launch of LYTENAVA™ (bevacizumab gamma) in Germany and the UK for wet AMD treatment. They are on track for a potential FDA approval in the US with a Prescription Drug User Fee Act goal date of August 27, 2025, for ONS-5010. They continue to work on launching LYTENAVA™ in Europe and are collaborating with Cencora for global commercialization. Financially, they reported a net loss of $46.4 million, with $7.6 million in cash and cash equivalents as of March 31, 2025. The company is optimistic about the future of ONS-5010/LYTENAVA™ and their goals for enhancing the standard of care in retina diseases.
news.headline: Outlook Therapeutics plans commercial launches of LYTENAVA in Germany and UK, aiming for FDA approval for ONS-5010 by August 27, 2025. Reported net loss of $46.4 million for Q2 fiscal year 2025.

news.summary: vTv Therapeutics Inc. is conducting a Phase 3 trial for cadisegliatin in patients with type 1 diabetes (T1D), with topline data expected in the second half of 2026. The company reinitiated screening for the trial and successfully submitted a protocol amendment to shorten the study duration to 6 months from 12, without impacting the original key endpoints. The company's cash position was reported at $31.1 million as of March 31, 2025, with R&D expenses of $2.8 million and G&A expenses of $3.7 million for the first quarter of 2025. The net loss attributable to vTv shareholders for the same period was $5.1 million. alertController therapeutics Inc. Condensed Consolidated Balance Sheets show total assets at $32,027 as of March 31, 2025.
news.headline: vTv Therapeutics reports $31.1 million cash position in Q1 2025. Phase 3 data for cadisegliatin in T1D expected by 2026.

news.summary: Century Therapeutics, Inc. has started patient dosing in Phase 1 CALiPSO-1 trial for autoimmune disease treatment with CNTY-101, expanding to U.S. and European sites post-CTA authorizations in Germany, France, and Italy. CNTY-308 is due to enter IND-enabling studies mid-2025 for B-cell autoimmune diseases and malignancies. Financially, they reported $185.8 million in cash as of March 31, 2025, and collaboration revenue of $109.2 million for Q1 2025. R&D expenses were $26.6 million, and net income was $76.6 million. They aim to deliver clinical data for CNTY-101 by the end of 2025. The company showcased preclinical pipeline advancements at ASGCT 28th Annual Meeting, focusing on iPSC-derived cell therapies for autoimmune diseases and cancer, emphasizing 'tunable' CD4+/CD8+ ab T cells and Allo-Evasion™ 5.0 technology. Their commitment to developing off-the-shelf cell therapies aims at expanding patient access to treatment options.
news.headline: Century Therapeutics reported Q1 2025 results: $185.8m cash, patient dosing in CALiPSO-1 trial, initiation of CNTY-308 IND studies.

news.summary: Katapult Holdings, Inc., an e-commerce-focused financial technology company, reported its first-quarter financial results for 2025, with gross originations of $64.2 million, a 15.4% increase year-over-year, and total revenue of $71.9 million, a 10.6% increase. Operating expenses increased by 17.3%, leading to a net loss of $5.7 million for the quarter. Adjusted EBITDA was $2.2 million, down from $5.6 million in the first quarter of 2024. The company aims to achieve a 25-30% year-over-year increase in gross originations and a 17-20% revenue increase in the second quarter of 2025. It also reiterated its goal of at least 20% growth in gross originations for the full year 2025. Katapult plans to introduce new merchants to its app marketplace in 2025 and expects revenue growth to be at least 20%. The company ended the quarter with $14.3 million in cash and $77.8 million in outstanding debt. Katapult highlighted its continued focus on customer satisfaction and repeat customer engagement.
news.headline: Katapult Holdings, Inc. reported 15.4% gross originations growth and 10.6% revenue growth in Q1 2025, above outlook. Expects 25-30% gross originations growth, 17-20% revenue growth, and breakeven EBITDA in Q2 2025. Maintained full-year 2025 guidance of 20% gross originations, revenue growth. Net loss for Q1 was $5.7 million, adjusted EBITDA was $2.2 million. Total revenue was $71.9 million, with total cash and equivalents of $14.3 million. 57.4% of gross originations came from repeat customers. The company also set a long-term target range of write-offs as 8% to 10% of revenue. Key metrics include gross originations, total revenue, gross profit, adjusted gross profit, and adjusted EBITDA. Full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Alliance Entertainment Holding Corporation reported a $1.9 million net income for the third quarter ended March 31, 2025, showing a $5.3 million year-over-year improvement. They reduced revolver debt by 25% year-over-year. Direct-to-consumer sales reached 35% of gross revenue. Some highlights include an exclusive home entertainment license agreement with Paramount Pictures, expanded retail distribution of Handmade by Robots, and upcoming releases from iconic franchises. Physical movie sales surged 39%, reaching $58 million, while vinyl record sales increased by 11% to $86 million. Consumer Direct Fulfillment sales made up 35% of gross revenue. Inventory levels reduced to $93.2 million, down 13% from the previous year. Operating expenses decreased by 11.4% year-over-year. Interest expense dropped by 20.2%. Gross profit margin increased to 13.6%. For the nine months ended March 31, 2025, net income was $9.3 million, up 349% from the same period in 2024. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $4.9 million, a 66% year-over-year increase, and $24.4 million for the nine months, up 9.9% year-over-year. Alliance's CEO expressed confidence in capitalizing on trends in physical media, direct-to-consumer fulfillment, and collectibles for long-term growth. Net revenues for the quarter were $213.0 million, up 1% from the previous year, while gross profit was $29.1 million, up 3.7%. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $4.9 million, showing a 66% increase year-over-year. For the nine months, net revenues were $835.7 million, and gross profit was $96.9 million. Net income for the nine months was $9.3 million. Alliance Entertainment remains optimistic about future growth opportunities and value creation for shareholders.
news.headline: Alliance Entertainment reports $1.9 million net income in Q3 FY2025 with $213 million in revenue and $29.1 million in gross profit.

news.summary: Intermap Technologies reported an impressive 153% revenue growth in the first quarter of 2025, reaching $4.3 million compared to $1.7 million in the same period in 2024. The company's pro-forma adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 28%, up from 25% in the first quarter of 2024. Additionally, their projected revenue for 2025 is expected to be between $30–35 million with the same 28% EBITDA margin.Intermap highlighted significant achievements in the government sector, including being selected as a vendor for the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency's $200 million Luno B IDIQ contract, increasing their addressable opportunity to $500 million. The company also successfully executed Phase 1 of Indonesia's national mapping initiative, exceeding specifications and positioning themselves well for continued participation in Phase 2 of the $653 million World Bank-funded ILASP project.Intermap's investments to support permitting and pursuit costs for follow-on awards, as well as their focus on strengthening partnerships and federal contracts, have contributed to their positive financial results. The company's total assets grew to $19.2 million from $11.9 million at the end of 2024, and their cash, unbilled, and accounts receivable totaled $13.9 million compared to $6.5 million at the end of 2024.Intermap's Chairman and CEO, Patrick A. Blott, expressed confidence in the company's growth prospects and recurring revenue potential, affirming their 2025 guidance. A conference call is scheduled to further discuss these results.
news.headline: Intermap reports 153% revenue growth in Q1 2025, reaching $4.3 million with 28% EBITDA margin. Confirms 2025 revenue projection of $30-35 million.

news.summary: Bit Digital, Inc. announced their financial results for the First Quarter of 2025, with total revenue at $25.1 million, a 17% decrease from the previous year. Bitcoin mining revenue was $7.8 million, down by 64%, while Cloud services revenue increased by 84% to $14.8 million. Colocation services revenue reached $1.6 million and ETH staking revenue was at $0.6 million. The Company had $61.3 million in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash, and total assets of $458.2 million with Shareholders' Equity of $417.4 million as of March 31, 2025. Adjusted EBITDA was $(44.5) million compared to $58.5 million for Q1 2024. The Company earned 83.3 bitcoins and 211.0 ETH in the first quarter of 2025. They had 20,854 miners in operation with a total maximum hash rate of 2.4 EH/s. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bit Digital, Inc. reported Q1 2025 results with $25.1M revenue, a 17% YoY decrease. Bitcoin mining revenue dropped 64% to $7.8M, cloud services revenue surged 84% to $14.8M, and colocation revenue was $1.6M. ETH staking revenue was $0.6M. The company had $61.3M in cash and $141.4M in total liquidity. Adjusted EBITDA was $(44.5M) compared to $58.5M in Q1 2024. The company earned 83.3 bitcoins and 211.0 ETH in Q1 2025. Active hash rate for bitcoin mining fleet was 1.5 EH/s. Treasury holds 417.6 BTC, 24,434.2 ETH valued at $34.5M and $44.5M respectively.

news.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. reported strong financial results for the second quarter ended April 27, 2025, with a revenue of $7.10 billion, showing a 7 percent increase year over year. The company saw a record GAAP EPS of $2.63 and a record non-GAAP EPS of $2.39, up 28 percent and 14 percent respectively. Operating margins were at 30.5 percent GAAP and 30.7 percent non-GAAP. Cash flow from operations was $1.57 billion, with $2.00 billion distributed to shareholders. The company's gross margins were at 49.1 percent GAAP and 49.2 percent non-GAAP. Applied Materials emphasized its positioning in major technology advancements and strong performance across its business segments including Semiconductor Systems, Applied Global Services, and Display. The company's outlook for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 projects a total net revenue of around $7.2 billion, a non-GAAP gross margin of 48.3 percent, and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $2.35. About Applied Materials: Applied Materials, Inc. is a leader in materials engineering solutions for producing chips and displays globally. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Applied Materials reported Q2 results with revenue at $7.10B, GAAP EPS at $2.63, up 28% YoY. Operating margin at 30.5%.

news.summary: NRx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is making significant advancements in its plan to gain FDA approval for two new drugs and establish a network of clinics specializing in neuroplastic therapies for treating severe depression and PTSD. The company recently completed the third tranche of a convertible note offering and registered direct equity offering, securing cash to support operations through the end of 2025. NRx-100, a preservative-free IV ketamine, is set to complete its New Drug Application (NDA) filing in Q2 2025, with the FDA waiving the $4.3 million NDA submission fee. NRx-101 is also on track for NDA submission in 2025. HOPE Therapeutics is expanding its clinic network, with planned acquisitions estimated to generate around $15 million in revenue. The company has entered negotiations with additional clinical entities potentially representing $20 million in revenue. Additionally, a strategic investment and debt financing totaling $10.3 million have been secured to support these acquisitions. Jonathan Javitt, MD, MPH, Chairman and CEO of NRx Pharmaceuticals, expressed confidence in the company's progress and dedication to mental health innovation. NRx-100 is designed to address the national crisis of suicidal depression, with the Company making strides in stability testing and regulatory filings to bring the drug to market. The Company is also working on an Abbreviated New Drug Application (aNDA) for existing ketamine indications and has filed a U.S. patent application for NRX-100, potentially providing exclusivity until 2045.ruptcy services for strategic growth opportunities.
news.headline: NRx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: NRXP) reports Q1 2025 financial results. Focused on FDA approval for 2 drugs, developing mental health clinics. Clinics expected to generate $15M in revenue, with $20M potential. NRX-100 to complete NDA filing in Q2 2025, $4.3M fee waived. NRX-101 on track for NDA submission in 2025. news.summary: Eli Lilly And Co (LLY) has announced a strategic partnership with Rznomics Inc., a South Korea-based biopharmaceutical company focused on RNA-based therapeutics. This collaboration aims to develop and commercialize RNA-editing therapies utilizing Rznomics' trans-splicing ribozyme platform. The partnership is centered around creating RNA-editing treatments for sensorineural hearing loss. Rznomics will lead the early-stage research based on agreed-upon plans, while Lilly will take charge of the subsequent development and commercialization efforts. This collaboration signifies a significant step in advancing therapeutic solutions, with market analysts expressing positive views on Eli Lilly's stock performance.
news.headline: Eli Lilly partners with Rznomics on $1.3 billion deal for hearing loss treatment using RNA-editing therapeutics.
